layman_headline: Fear grips crypto markets — no clear setup worth the risk right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.2%, which means money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. None of the coins on the watchlist present a compelling enough setup to justify opening a new position. Some coins like ZRO are approaching oversold territory, but their charts are still bearish with no signs of a reversal yet. Patience is the play here.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines and wait. The market mood is fearful and altcoins are getting beaten down. A better entry will come when we see actual reversal signals, not just cheap prices.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all candidates reveals a weak opportunity set. GRASS has the sharpest 7-day pullback (-24.4%) but shows a bearish engulfing candle and chart bias of -0.9 — classic falling knife. ZRO's RSI at 31 is the most interesting signal given our track record shows RSI-based entries have a 75% win rate, but the chart is in a confirmed downtrend with price below all MAs. HTX has the strongest chart bias (+1.1) with a bullish Marubozu, but RSI at 58 flags overbought risk and the token's micro-cap nature and already-extended 5.2% weekly gain reduce conviction. ETHFI shows bullish momentum (+13.5% 5-day) but resistance is only 2.5% away with poor R/R of 0.11x. With Fear & Greed at 26 (improving trend) and BTC dominance at 56.2%, this is an environment that punishes altcoin entries. Better to wait for ZRO to confirm a base near $0.85-0.88 support or for Fear & Greed to cross above 30.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to drain liquidity from altcoins — any further rise would crush alt positions
Fear & Greed at 26 could deteriorate further if U.S.-Iran conflict escalates, triggering broader risk-off moves
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital leaving the crypto ecosystem entirely
watch_list: ZRO RSI dropping below 30 could signal a capitulation bottom — watch for RSI divergence or a hammer candle at $0.85 support
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from weekly avg of 24 to 26; a move above 30 would signal safer entry conditions
Bitcoin's test of Fidelity's power law support line — a bounce here could lift the entire market and create alt opportunities
market_context: Bitcoin is holding near $64K but the broader market is fearful (F&G 26) with high BTC dominance at 56.2%. Stablecoin outflows and geopolitical tensions from U.S.-Iran strikes are keeping risk appetite suppressed, making this a poor environment for new altcoin entries.
context_banner: Market fear and high BTC dominance create a hostile environment for altcoins — discipline demands patience over action.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with improving trend from 24 weekly average — not yet at levels that historically produce reliable bounce entries.
ZRO RSI at 31 is the most oversold candidate, but needs confirmation of a reversal (hammer candle, RSI divergence) before entry is warranted.
Stablecoin market cap shrinking $10B since May and Bitcoin nearing power law support both signal macro uncertainty that keeps altcoin risk elevated.
Bitcoin nearing 'late stages of bear market' per Jamie Coutts suggests a macro bottom may be forming, but timing an entry is premature without technical confirmation.
Best chart bias is HTX at +1.1, but the majority of candidates show bearish or neutral chart structures — the market is not offering clean setups.
